task.description: Repair active_member_ids so it accepts member id aliases id, member_id, user_id, and nested member.id. Active status tokens are active, enabled, yes, 1, or True after trimming and case-normalizing strings; all other or missing statuses are inactive. Trim ids and skip blank or missing ids. For duplicate canonical ids, choose the status row by source priority first (manual > directory > import > missing/other), then by greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, then by later input order. Return the ids whose winning row is active, sorted lexicographically.

task.description: Repair totals_by_project so it returns numeric totals by project id. Read project ids from project_id, projectId, project, or nested project.id, trim project ids, and skip blank or missing project ids. If an expense_id is present, duplicate expense_ids are revisions: keep only the row with the greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, breaking timestamp ties by later input order, before applying status and totals. Ignore rows whose trimmed and case-normalized status is rejected or void after revision selection. Parse numeric amounts from numbers or numeric strings; missing or blank amount is 0. Preserve first-seen project order based on accepted rows in the returned dict.

Repair normalize_events so it returns events sorted by id with exact output fields in this order: id, title, kind, and public. Read ids from id, event_id, or metadata.id and skip blank or missing ids. Read title/kind from top-level fields or metadata aliases title/name and kind/type, trim title and kind, title-case title, and lowercase kind. Read public from top-level public or metadata public/visibility and default missing public to False. Public tokens 'public', 'yes', '1', and True mean True. Rows with status deleted/remove or deleted set to True are tombstones. Merge duplicate ids by setting public True if any non-tombstone duplicate is public. For title and kind, use the nonblank value from the greatest numeric updated_at timestamp; rows without updated_at behave as timestamp 0, and ties are broken by later input order. If the greatest timestamp row for an id is a tombstone, omit that event.

task.description: Repair preference_flags so it resolves layered preference rows into normalized booleans. Read preference names from name, preference, key, or setting aliases; trim and lowercase names, and skip blank or missing names. Values 'on', 'enabled', 'yes', '1', 1, and True mean True; all other values, missing values, blank strings, and None mean False. Rows may include scope global, group, or user; scope priority is user > group > global > missing. Within the same scope, choose by greatest numeric updated_at timestamp, then later input order. A row with locked True prevents lower-priority scopes from changing that preference, but can be overridden by a later row in the same scope or any higher-priority scope. Return preferences in first-seen normalized name order.

Repair category_paths so it normalizes a category tree from mixed rows. Each row is either a string path or a dict with path, category, label, or name. Split paths on '>' or '/', trim and collapse whitespace in each segment, lowercase segments, and skip blank segments. Dict rows may include aliases mapping raw segment names to canonical segment names; apply aliases case-insensitively after trimming. Dict rows with deleted True or status deleted/remove are tombstones: remove that exact normalized path and all descendant paths seen so far, and prevent later rows from re-adding that path or any descendant path unless the tombstoned path is later seen with revive True. Preserve first-seen sibling order under each parent and return full paths joined by ' > ' in preorder. Skip malformed rows without a usable string path.
